stories.title: Knicks 107-106 Spurs — OG Anunoby's Tip-In Completes Largest Comeback in NBA Finals History
stories.angle: New York trailed 76-49 at halftime — the largest road halftime deficit in Finals history — before mounting the greatest comeback in Finals history; OG Anunoby tipped in Jalen Brunson's miss with 1.2 seconds left to win 107-106; Brunson finished with 36 points, Anunoby a playoff career-high 33; Spurs shot 8-of-39 in the second half after going 28-of-47 in the first; Knicks now lead series 3-1

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
DOWN 29 AT HALFTIME.

OG Anunoby tip-in with 1.2 seconds left.

Knicks 107, Spurs 106.

The LARGEST comeback in NBA Finals history. Series lead: 3-1. #KnicksTape #NBAFinals

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Spurs: 76 pts on 59.6% in the first half.
Spurs: 30 pts on 20.5% in the second half.

A 46-point swing. Tied for the largest in the shot clock era.

Brunson 36. Anunoby 33 (career high). Knicks lead 3-1. #KnicksTape #NBAFinals

article.headline: Knicks 107, Spurs 106: OG Anunoby's Tip-In Caps the Largest Comeback in NBA Finals History

research.facts:
Game 4 Recap — Knicks 107-106 Spurs
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Final score: Knicks 107, Spurs 106 (June 10, Madison Square Garden)
- Series: Knicks lead 3-1
- Jalen Brunson: 36 points (lead scorer)
- OG Anunoby: 33 points (playoff career-high), 7 three-pointers (playoff career-high), hit game-winning tip-in with 1.2 seconds left
- Spurs halftime lead: 76-49 (largest road halftime lead in Finals history, excl. 2020 bubble per CBS Sports)
- Spurs first half: 28-of-47 (59.6%) from field; scored 76 points
- Spurs second half: 8-of-39 (20.5%) from field; scored 30 points
- 46-point swing from first to second half tied for largest in shot clock era (Elias Sports Bureau)
- OG Anunoby tipped in Brunson's long 3-point attempt at 1.2 seconds remaining
- Comeback of 29 points is the largest in NBA Finals history (previous record: 24 points)
research.sources:
- Source: ESPN, NBA.com, CBS Sports (multiple confirming)
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Spurs' 14 three-pointers in the first half set a Finals first-half record (per CBS Sports, single source)
- This came as "only the second time in Finals history" that road team won Games 1-3; now NYK lead 3-1
**Research notes:**
- Game started as total Spurs domination; Wemby and company could not miss in H1
- Knicks mounted comeback in second half; MSG erupted
- Anunoby catch the ball off Brunson's 3-point miss and tipped it with 1.2 seconds left
- Spurs' second-half shooting collapsed dramatically

stories.title: Game 5 Preview — Knicks One Win Away From First Championship Since 1973
stories.angle: Game 5 is Saturday June 13, 8:30 PM ET on ABC in San Antonio; Spurs are 5.5-point favorites despite facing elimination; Knicks went 2-0 on the road to open this series and can clinch the title in San Antonio; only one team in NBA history (2016 Warriors) has blown a 3-1 lead in the Finals; Wembanyama needs a response after going 9-of-25 in Game 4

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Knicks lead 3-1. Game 5 is Saturday in San Antonio. 8:30 PM ET. ABC.

Only one team in NBA history has ever blown a 3-1 Finals lead.

Spurs are 5.5-point favorites. The market still believes.

Does it matter? #KnicksTape #NBAFinals

research.facts:
Game 5 Preview — June 13, San Antonio
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Game 5: Saturday June 13, 8:30 PM ET, ABC, Frost Bank Center, San Antonio
- Knicks lead series 3-1
- Spurs listed as 5.5-point favorites per ESPN/DraftKings (per CBS Sports/heavy.com)
- Knicks went 2-0 to open the series in San Antonio (Games 1 and 2)
- Knicks can clinch the title on the road in San Antonio
- Only team to blow a 3-1 Finals lead in NBA history: 2016 Golden State Warriors (lost to LeBron's Cavaliers)
- So 37 of 38 teams with a 3-1 Finals lead have closed it out
- Wembanyama: 24 pts, 9-25 FG (36%), 13 reb, 3 blk in Game 4
**Research notes:**
- Spurs favorites because Frost Bank Center; have been strong at home in playoffs
- Knicks proved they can win in SA; this is the same venue where they won Games 1 and 2
- Historical precedent very strongly in Knicks' favor (3-1 leads almost never blown)

stories.title: Giannis Trade Expected Before June 23 Draft — Heat Still Frontrunner
stories.angle: League sources now have increasing belief a Giannis trade will happen before the June 23 draft; Miami still the frontrunner; the Bucks reportedly waited to see if the Knicks would blow the 2-0 Finals lead before making a move; with New York on the brink of a title, the calculus changes; June 23 deadline remains

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
New from league sources: increasing belief the Giannis trade happens before June 23.

The Bucks were reportedly waiting to see if the Knicks would blow a 2-0 Finals lead.

They didn't. The clock is now ticking. #NBA

research.facts:
Giannis Trade — Heat Frontrunner, Deal Expected Before Draft
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Miami Heat remain the frontrunner for Giannis
- "Increasing belief" in league circles a deal happens before June 23 draft (per CBS Sports)
- Bucks reportedly waited to see if Knicks would blow Finals lead before deciding on timing (per CBS Sports)
- June 23 draft is the deadline Bucks co-owner Haslam wants a decision by
- OKC Thunder not interested
- Celtics — no direct talks (per Windhorst/ESPN)
- $275M extension available October 1
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Giannis reportedly prefers Miami as destination (Yardbarker/Fischer/Stein, single-source compound claim)
- Giannis has concerns about Heat's roster quality after gutting it to get him

stories.title: Wembanyama's Collapse — What Went Wrong in Game 4's Second Half
stories.angle: Wembanyama led the Spurs with 24 points and 13 rebounds but shot 9-of-25 (36%); the Spurs as a team went from 76 first-half points to just 30 in the second half — a 46-point swing that tied the largest collapse in the shot clock era; the 22-year-old's reaction: "It just hurts"; can he reset for an elimination Game 5?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama after Game 4:

"It just hurts."

24 pts. 9-of-25. 13 rebounds. 44 minutes.

The Spurs scored 76 in the first half. 30 in the second.

Can he reset one more time? Game 5. Saturday. #GoSpursGo #NBAFinals

article.headline: Wembanyama After Historic Collapse: "It Just Hurts" — Can He Respond in Elimination Game 5?

research.facts:
Wembanyama's Game 4 Collapse
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Wembanyama Game 4: 24 pts, 9-25 FG, 2-8 3PT, 4-7 FT, 13 reb, 3 blk, 1 ast in 44 minutes
- Quote: "It just hurts" — post-game per olympics.com/news
- Spurs second half: 8-39 FG (20.5%), 30 pts
- Spurs first half: 28-47 FG (59.6%), 76 pts
- 46-point halftime-to-second-half swing: tied largest in shot clock era (Elias Sports Bureau per CBS Sports)
- Spurs led by as many as 29 points before Knicks comeback
**Research notes:**
- Wemby had one of the highest-scoring first halves by any team in Finals history going with him
- His individual performance was adequate but the team's collapse was historic
- Age 22, first Finals; question is whether he can do what he did in Game 3 (32 pts after errant pass)

stories.title: AJ Dybantsa — 12 Days Left and Wizards Meeting Scheduled
stories.angle: 12 days until June 23 NBA Draft at Barclays Center; Wizards plan to host Dybantsa for a pre-draft visit in the coming days; despite his "100%" proclamation, the Combine revealed unexpected uncertainty — sources say Darryn Peterson remains "in the mix" at No. 1; Dybantsa posted a 42-inch vertical and 7-foot wingspan at the Combine

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
12 days to the 2026 NBA Draft.

Wizards are scheduling a pre-draft meeting with AJ Dybantsa.

But CBS Sports says it's "not the lock everyone assumed."

Darryn Peterson is still in the mix at No. 1. #NBADraft

article.headline: Dybantsa to No. 1 Is No Longer a Lock: Wizards Meeting Scheduled as Draft Uncertainty Rises

research.facts:
AJ Dybantsa — Wizards Meeting, Uncertainty
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Draft: June 23-24, Barclays Center, Brooklyn (12 days away)
- Dybantsa: 25.5 PPG (Division I high), 51.0% FG, 6'9" / 210 lbs, BYU
- NBA Combine: 42-inch vertical, 7-foot-0.5 wingspan
- Wizards plan to host Dybantsa for pre-draft visit "in the coming days" (per The Athletic via roundtable.io)
- Dybantsa previously said he's "100%" sure he's going No. 1 to Washington (Bleacher Report)
- Darryn Peterson "still in the mix at No. 1" according to CBS Sports Combine intel
- Wizards have not officially committed to Dybantsa
- Wizards record: 17-65; first No. 1 pick since John Wall 2010
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Peterson expected No. 2 to Utah Jazz if Wizards take Dybantsa (mock drafts, multiple sources)
- Cameron Boozer projected No. 3 (multiple mock drafts)
